identificationModule.briefTitle: Evaluation of the Exoskeleton ATLAS 2030 as Robot-assisted Physical Therapy to Children With Neuromuscular Diseases
identificationModule.officialTitle: Evaluation of the Safety of ATLAS 2030 in the Application of Robot-assisted Physical Therapy to Children With Neuromuscular Diseases Phase I

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study aims to evaluate the safety, usability and acceptability of a motorized mobility assistance exoskeleton. The protocol explores the use of the motorized device during static and dynamic rehabilitation sessions focused on walking and functional activities, with the expectation of evaluating the safety and usability of the device in the population studied. The protocol has been focused on defining how the device can be used appropriately in this population in a safe and effective manner by rehabilitation specialists. The study also aims to assess the safety of clinical staff who implement the intervention.

armGroups.label: Intervention Group
armGroups.description: 9 treatment sessions will be performed with the ATLAS2030 exoskeleton. The rehabilitation sessions last approximately 90 minutes. Two sessions are scheduled per week, for two weeks.

interventions.name: Exoskeleton rehabilitation
interventions.description: The participants will perform physical therapy with the ATLSA2030 device, for 90 minutes per session.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Safety as number of serious device adverse events
primaryOutcomes.description: Presence of a serious device adverse events where the participant or therapist is involved
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: after each use of exoskeleton, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Safety as presence of adverse events or adverse device events
secondaryOutcomes.description: Presence of a device adverse events where the participant or therapist is involved.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: after each use of exoskeleton, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Exercises [measured as time per exercise in seconds]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Measured as time per event for at each therapy session.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: after each use of exoskeleton, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Transfers [measured as time to carry out the transfers in seconds]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Measured as time to carry out the transfers
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At each use of exoskeleton, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Acceptability [number of participants which discontinue the participation during the trial in relation with the included participants]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Measured as abandon ratio
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the end of the study, at the 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Accessibility [number of potential participants in relation with the included participants]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Measured as relation between number of participants and number of potential participants which weren't recruited
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the end of the study, at the 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Fall Prevalence [number of falls during the using of the device]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Number of falling events ocurred from the participant or therapist
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: During the use of the device, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Skin integrity [number of skin injuries related to the device]
secondaryOutcomes.description: skin integrity measured as the number of skin injuries
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: before and after each use of exoskeleton, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Pain [Wong-Baker FACES Pain Rating Scale]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Pain registered before and after the use of the device, by the participant and therapist. The options are from 1 to 6. A higher score means a worse outcome.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Fatigue [Borg Rating Scale of Perceived Exertion]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Fatigue registered before and after the use of the device, by the participant and therapist. Measured from 0 to 10, being 0 as fully rested and 10 very tired. A higher score means a worse outcome.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Spasticity [Modified Ashworth Scale]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Spasticity registered before and after the use of the device
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Heart rate [sphygmomanometer]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Number of heart bets per minute
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Physical evaluation [number of physical injuries detected]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Physical Evaluation as presence of tissue damage
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Muscle Strength [measured with Hand Held Dynamometer in N]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Muscle Strength measured at hip, knee and ankle muscles
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the first day, 3rd week and 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: ROM [measured with goniometer]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Range of Movement
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the first day, 3rd week and 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Functional Mobility [Functional Ambulation Category scale]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Functional Mobility with and without the device
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the first day, 3rd week and 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: System Usability [System Usability Scale]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Measurement of System's Usability measured by the therapist
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the end of the study, 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: User perception of the device [QUEST 2.0 and QUEST 2.1]
secondaryOutcomes.description: QUEST 2.0 will be assessed by the therapist, and QUEST 2.1 will be assessed by the participant
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the end of the study, 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Distance covered walking [6MWT]
secondaryOutcomes.description: 6MWT recorded using the device
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the first day, 3rd week and 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Device malfunction [as number and type of device malfunction]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Any device malfunction will be recorded
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: During the use of the device, for 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Particpant's stability [Functional Reach Test]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Functional Reach Test
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At the first day, 3rd week and 5th week

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Systolic/Diastolic Pressure [sphygmomanometer]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Systolic Pressure and Diastolic Pressure measured with sphygmomanometer (measured in mmHg)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Breath Rate [number of breaths per minute]
secondaryOutcomes.description: Number of breaths per minute
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.measure: SpO2 [pulse oximeter]
secondaryOutcomes.description: SpO2 measured as %
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after the use of the device, during 5 weeks

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Maximum user weight of 35 kg
* Thigh length (distance from the greater trochanter to the lateral condyle of the tibia) from 24 cm to 40 cm.
* Leg length (distance from the lateral condyle of the tibia to the lateral malleolus) from 23 to 39 cm.
* Pelvis width (between greater trochanters) from 24 to 35 cm.
* Ability to achieve ankle dorsiflexion to 90˚
* 20º or more hip flessum.
* 20º or more knee flessum.
* No allergies to any of the ATLAS materials: cotton, nylon, polyester, polyamide, polyethylene or propylene.
* Informed consent signed by legal guardians.
* Confirmed diagnosis of cerebral palsy or neuromuscular disease affecting walking ability at early development stages
* Stable medical condition with no changes in disease-specific medication in the last 6 months, and additional medication in the last month.
* Patient being followed according to the recommended standards for his or her illness
* Ability to maintain, spontaneously or with a brace, the head and trunk while standing and walking.
* No need for daytime ventilation (have an oxygen saturation and PCO2 with normal ambient oxygen
* Score on the FAC scale between 1 and 4 points
* Not being able to walk without assistance

Exclusion Criteria:

* Patient's inability to follow simple instructions and/or communicate discomfort.
* Invasive or non-invasive daytime ventilation.
* Orthostatic hypotension.
* 20º or more hip flessum.
* 20º or more knee flessum
* Scoliosis with a Cobb angle of more than 25º that cannot wear a corset during the test.
* Severe skin injury to the lower extremities.
* Scheduled surgery (spine, extremities) during the period of the study or surgery performed (spine, extremities) in the last 6 months.
* History of fracture without trauma.
* History of traumatic bone fracture in lower limbs or pelvic girdle in the last 3 months
* Not receiving regular standing rehabilitation sessions.
* Absence of control of the head or trunk in an upright position without the possibility of wearing a corset during the use of the exoskeleton
* Refusal of the patient or legal guardian to include the child in the study.
* Skin problems (diseases, allergies, sensitivity ...) that prevent the use of exoskeleton accessories on the skin of patients.

references.citation: Cumplido-Trasmonte C, Ramos-Rojas J, Delgado-Castillejo E, Garces-Castellote E, Puyuelo-Quintana G, Destarac-Eguizabal MA, Barquin-Santos E, Plaza-Flores A, Hernandez-Melero M, Gutierrez-Ayala A, Martinez-Moreno M, Garcia-Armada E. Effects of ATLAS 2030 gait exoskeleton on strength and range of motion in children with spinal muscular atrophy II: a case series. J Neuroeng Rehabil. 2022 Jul 19;19(1):75. doi: 10.1186/s12984-022-01055-x.

ancestors.term: Congenital, Hereditary, and Neonatal Diseases and Abnormalities
